name: Chocolate Praline Layer Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 Pillsbury Devil's Food cake mix - pkg
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. In a small heavy saucepan, combine butter, whipping cream, and brown sugar. Cook over low heat until butter is melted, stirring occasionally. Pour into two 8 or 9-inch round cake pans; sprinkle evenly with chopped pecans.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cake mix, water, oil, and eggs. Mix on low speed until moistened; beat at high speed for 2 minutes.
4. Carefully spoon batter over the pecan mixture in the cake pans.
5. Bake at 325°F for 35 to 45 minutes or until a cake tester springs back lightly when touched in the center. Allow cooling for 5 minutes, then remove from pans and cool completely.
6. In a small bowl, beat whipping cream until soft peaks form. Blend in powdered sugar and vanilla; beat until stiff peaks form.
7. Assemble the cake by placing one layer on a serving plate, praline side up. Spread with half of the whipped cream. Top with the second layer, praline side up, and spread the remaining whipping cream on top. Garnish with whole pecans and chocolate curls if desired. Store in the refrigerator.
